feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Mass General Brigham, Dana-Farber dig into early cancer detection efforts — but how much is too much?
entry.summary: Doctors at the centers are coordinating not only which tests patients would benefit from, but what, if anything, they should do with the results.

entry.title: A tool to help keep dementia in check
entry.summary: Many people fear that a family history of dementia dooms them to inevitably suffer the condition themselves. But a new tool, the Brain Care Score, shows how lifestyle changes can be beneficial, cutting the risk of dementia.

entry.title: 4 subtle signs you aren’t eating enough protein
entry.summary: The macronutrient is important for maintaining muscle as you age, but it’s possible you’re not getting as much as you think.

entry.title: The body’s most mysterious organ may play a key role in longevity and cancer
entry.summary: A raft of research is recasting the thymus from a bit player to a potent regulator of aging and immune health.

entry.title: It's probably already in your fridge — and it could help in cutting risk of Alzheimer's
entry.summary: Eating seafood twice a week can be important to protect the brain from different diseases.

entry.title: ‘A tough one.’ Boston’s allergy season came late but is hitting hard.
entry.summary: Doctors said itchy, watery eyes, symptoms of allergic conjunctivitis, are especially bad this year.

entry.title: “Not just living longer, but living better”—a certified trainer shares five things that matter most to aging well and longevity
entry.summary: Address these five factors now to stay younger as you age

entry.title: “You Are Just Fat”: 65 Patients Who Couldn’t Believe The Treatment They Got In The ER
entry.summary: If you're heading to the emergency room, there's a good chance you're not doing it just for kicks. It's not exactly a fun place to hang out on a Friday night and make new friends.

entry.title: Two Supplements For Menopause May Actually Help, Expert Reveals
entry.summary: Social media is saturated with menopause solutions: powders for brain fog, gummies for sleep, or capsules promising hormonal balance.

entry.title: Brain scans of 800 incarcerated men link psychopathy to an expanded cortical surface area
entry.summary: A large-scale brain imaging study of over 800 incarcerated men reveals that highly psychopathic individuals possess an expanded cortical surface area and a compressed physical brain organization, offering new clues into the biology of empathy and antisocial b…

entry.title: World-First Study Reveals Human Hearts Can Regenerate After a Heart Attack
entry.summary: For the first time, scientists have shown that heart muscle cells can regrow after a heart attack in humans, a process that had previously only been observed in mice.

entry.title: The Roots of Dementia Trace Back All The Way to Childhood, Experts Reveal
entry.summary: Dementia is often associated with older people, but it doesn't just appear out of nowhere.

entry.title: How to sleep better, from bedtimes to regularity – what the research says
entry.summary: Sleep research has come a long way in the last decade. Here’s what you need to know to help make your slumber better.

entry.title: This Simple Home Device May Boost Brain Power in Adults Over 40
entry.summary: HEPA air purifiers can slightly boost brain function in adults 40+, potentially reducing pollution-related cognitive decline. Using a HEPA air purifier for one month may modestly improve cognitive performance in adults over 40, particularly in areas like ment…

entry.title: Scientists Develop New Antibody For Virus That Infects 95% of People
entry.summary: The Epstein-Barr virus (EBV) is one of the world's most common viruses, thought to be lurking in 95 percent of adults.

entry.title: Omega-3 Supplements May Increase Risk of Cognitive Decline, Scientists Warn
entry.summary: Nutritional supplements are exploding in popularity, but some experts remain skeptical of the numerous health claims surrounding these pills and their safety at high dosages.

entry.title: What Eating Yogurt Every Day Does to Your Body, According to Nutrition Experts
entry.summary: Eating yogurt every day will benefit your bones, gut, and muscle growth, say nutrition experts. It will also support muscle repair and satiety, helping you stay full for longer. For the most health benefits, the recommend plain and unflavored yogurt.

entry.title: Diagnosed with colon cancer at 36, she spent her last years helping others with the disease
entry.summary: The daughter of Iranian immigrants, Asal Sayas worked in the White House and Senate, lobbied for AIDS research and became a tireless champion for people with cancer.

entry.title: Yawning does something unexpected to your brain: MRI scans reveal how yawning and deep breathing differ | Health
entry.summary: While yawning and taking deep breaths are similar actions, they have distinct effects on the cerebrospinal fluid in the brain, according to a recent study. | Health

entry.title: Coffee helps protect your body from aging and disease, study says
entry.summary: Scientists have identified a mechanism through which coffee compounds can help the body against age-related decline.

entry.title: Longevity bros aren’t thinking enough about reducing chronic disease. But researchers are
entry.summary: Before trying to rewind the clock on aging, the best longevity approach for the most people may be preventing and treating chronic disease, experts say.

entry.title: How eating habits could influence Parkinson’s disease risk, according to experts
entry.summary: Observational research suggests that certain foods may lower or raise Parkinson's disease risk, though experts caution that diet is just one factor among many.

entry.title: Boosting one protein helps the brain fight Alzheimer’s
entry.summary: Scientists have discovered a way to help the brain clean itself of harmful Alzheimer’s plaques by activating its own support cells. By increasing a protein called Sox9, researchers were able to boost the activity of astrocytes, star shaped cells that help mai…

entry.title: The Most Effective Knee Arthritis Treatments Aren’t What You Expect
entry.summary: A sweeping analysis of nearly 10,000 patients challenges assumptions about how best to treat knee osteoarthritis.

entry.title: NYC hospitals gearing up for nightmarish World Cup diseases as fans descend on metro
entry.summary: Hospitals in New Jersey and New York, along with millions of their medical personnel, have been undergoing intense training for the past two years to deal with the 1.2 million people from around th…

entry.title: Scientists Develop Bioengineered Chewing Gum That Could Help Fight Oral Cancer
entry.summary: Bioengineered gum lowers HPV and harmful oral bacteria, offering a promising new approach for cancer prevention and treatment. Researchers led by Henry Daniell of the School of Dental Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania report that extracts from a bioe…

entry.title: Bees can detect viruses in food sources, but don't necessarily avoid them
entry.summary: The ability to detect viruses and other harmful pathogens is highly advantageous for animals, as it can guide their behavior and prevent them from illness, and—in severe cases—death. When it comes to species that live in organized groups, such as bees, ants a…

entry.title: Weight loss and hair loss: The growing hair treatment market from GLP-1s
entry.summary: A common side effect of many GLP-1 drugs is hair loss. It's creating a formerly untapped but growing market for hair treatment products.

entry.title: Sigmund Freud, Psychoanalyst: "The Favorite Son of a Mother Keeps for Life the Feeling of a Conqueror"
entry.summary: He was his mother's undisputed favorite. What Freud wrote about that in a private 1917 letter still rattles how we think about confidence today.

entry.title: Popular Weight-Loss Drugs Found To Cut Heart Attack and Stroke Risk
entry.summary: A large-scale analysis suggests that GLP-1 receptor agonists may do more than manage weight or blood sugar.

entry.title: Scientists sound alarm as dangerous amoebas spread globally
entry.summary: Free-living amoebae are emerging as a global health concern, fueled by warming temperatures and outdated water systems. While many are harmless, some can cause deadly infections and even protect other dangerous microbes. Their ability to survive heat and disi…

entry.title: Eye Supplementation: What Is It? And Why More Specialists Are Recommending It
entry.summary: Ophthalmologists finally have a supplement formula proven to delay blindness. The catch is knowing exactly when to take it and which version to trust.

entry.title: 7 Things Stroke Doctors Say You Should Never, Ever Do
entry.summary: Strokes are a leading cause of death in the United States — but there are ways you can lower your risk.

entry.title: Confirmed cases of measles in DC and Virginia as country faces significant resurgence
entry.summary: Officials with the District’s Department of Health announced a confirmed case of measles in the city on Thursday.

entry.title: Doctors told her to remove her uterus. The real cause of her pain lay elsewhere.
entry.summary: After years of worsening pain, irregular periods and soaring hormone levels, doctors discovered it wasn’t her reproductive organs driving her illness.

entry.title: Americans aren't sleeping enough. Here's what could help
entry.summary: Nearly a third of Americans get less than the recommended seven hours of sleep a night. A lot of us struggle to get to bed as we power through tasks or get lost in endless scrolling. Here's help.

entry.title: Scientists link daytime sleep-like brain waves to attention lapses in ADHD
entry.summary: For adults with ADHD, staying focused is often a daily struggle. New research suggests these attention lapses occur because the waking brain frequently slips into brief, sleep-like states, blurring the biological boundary between sleep and wakefulness.

entry.title: AI finds signs of pancreatic cancer before tumors develop
entry.summary: An artificial intelligence model from the Mayo Clinic detected early signs of possible pancreatic cancer on scans up to two years before patients were diagnosed.

entry.title: Nature vs nurture: How much of our personalities are determined at birth?
entry.summary: Research is revealing surprising new insights about how much our genes do – and don't – make us who we are.

entry.title: DC-area emergency rooms are seeing more patients with tick bites
entry.summary: The weekly rates of emergency room visits due to tick bites are the highest they’ve been since 2017, according to the CDC.

entry.title: Scientists Found Another Disturbing Thing Ultra-Processed Food Does to Your Body
entry.summary: Not too long ago, I wrote about an op-ed in the New York Times by a Georgetown professor arguing that “junk food” (a term he used for the ultra-processed slop that is TikTok and the like) is eroding our attention spans. Now, just a couple of weeks later, rese…

entry.title: Are oats really healthy for everyone? Doctors reveal hidden downsides you should know
entry.summary: Health News: Oats have become the face of “healthy eating.” From quick breakfasts to late-night snacks, they show up everywhere. Nutrition labels praise their fibr.

entry.title: Vitamin D's benefits: New findings on diabetes, IBD, and Alzheimer’s
entry.summary: In this study roundup, Medical News Today compiles the latest evidence on the benefits of Vitamin D, focusing on the key findings from 3 studies on diabetes, IBD, Alzheimer's disease.

entry.title: Surge in tick-borne illnesses prompts experts to urge precaution
entry.summary: SCOTIA — Linda Bucciferro remembers the day her life changed forever.

entry.title: Renown Children’s Hospital pediatricians advise on GI illnesses amid outbreaks at five WCSD schools
entry.summary: Renown Children’s Hospital pediatric experts are alerting parents and caregivers to an increase in gastrointestinal illness among children in several Reno-area schools.

entry.title: Beetroot juice isn't for everyone: Hidden side effects and why you should avoid it
entry.summary: You've probably heard the hype. Beetroot juice is good for blood pressure, it boosts athletic performance, it's packed with antioxidants. And sure, there's legitimate research backing some of that up. But here's what doesn't get mentioned nearly as much: beet…

entry.title: FDA expands access to a promising drug for one of the worst cancers
entry.summary: More patients will be able to take the pill while it is under review, as the agency cited the dire need for a drug to fight one of the most lethal cancers.

entry.title: Why Is No One Taking Baby Aspirin for Heart Disease Prevention Anymore?
entry.summary: A new report echoes what cardiologists are seeing with patients.

entry.title: New breakfast trend has Americans ditching cereal for soup as experts support it
entry.summary: Americans are now ditching cereal for breakfast, swapping it by starting their days with soup instead.

entry.title: Dane County sees surge in tick-related urgent care visits
entry.summary: MADISON, Wis. -- Tick season is off to a rough start in Dane County, with urgent care visits already doubling last year’s numbers, according to Public Health of Madison and
